Essential Job Duties
The Department of Food, Bioprocessing and Nutrition Sciences (
FBNS
) seeks applications from highly qualified scientists/engineers for a tenure-track assistant or associate professor position in food fermentation and bioprocessing sciences. This position carries an 80% Teaching and 20% Research split. The primary teaching focus includes fermentation and bioprocessing science courses for undergraduate and graduate students. In addition to teaching, you will be expected to develop and maintain an independent and innovative research program in food fermentation and bioprocessing science and secure extramural funding from federal, industry, and/or other sources such as foundations, commodity groups, etc.
In this role you will get to work in a collaborative and interdisciplinary manner with other units within and outside of the university, such as the Golden
LEAF
Biomanufacturing Training and Education Center (
BTEC
), Biological and Agricultural Engineering, Chemical Engineering, etc. You will be expected to mentor undergraduate students, graduate students, and post-docs in research. You will also be expected to serve on committees within the department, college, and/or university.
Key Responsibilities fall under these primary functions:
Teaching Responsibilities:
Teach at least 4 courses per year at the undergraduate and graduate levels, including bioprocessing science, fermentation science, and other courses as needed by the program. The courses consist of existing core bioprocessing science courses and develop core and advanced courses in fermentation sciences, including a general introductory level course and a specialized course with a focus on fermentation of either dairy, fruits/vegetables, wine, or spirits. Additionally you will also be involved designing hands-on laboratory modules in biomanufacturing, fermentation techniques, bioprocess monitoring and validation, and scale-up principles. You will also serve as director of undergraduate program for the bioprocessing degree program, which involves curriculum planning and review, annual assessment, and involvement with recruiting.
Research Program:
Establish their own line of scholarship that is complementary to, but not duplicative of, current
FBNS
faculty, and is expected to provide opportunities to mentor and engage undergraduate or graduate students in these research opportunities. The research program should address critical data gaps, new technologies, and/or stakeholder needs in the area of food fermentation and bioprocessing. The research focus for this position will center on advancing biomanufacturing and fermentation science. Some examples of key research areas include fermentation of fruits and vegetables, dairy, wine, and/or spirits systems; developing and optimizing bioprocesses and bioreactor systems for fermentation; upstream and downstream processing; engineering microbial consortia and synthetic communities (e.g., Lactobacillus, Saccharomyces, Starmerella) to enhance flavor, nutritional value, and shelf life of fermented foods and beverages.
NC State promotes an integrated approach to problem solving that transforms lives and provides leadership for social, economic, and technological development across North Carolina and around the world. NC State’s land grant mission of teaching, research, and service is dedicated to the service of North Carolina and its people. Applicants are encouraged to review the institution’s mission, vision, and strategic plan, and consider how their background, interest, and experience would enable them to support the university.
Other Responsibilities
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
A Ph.D. in Food Science, Bioprocessing, Chemical Engineering, Microbiology, or related field is required.
All degrees must be from appropriately accredited institutions.
Other Required Qualifications
Demonstrated experience in teaching or assisting with instruction in fermentation, bioprocessing, or a closely related discipline is required.
Experience with research related to bioprocess optimization, scale-up, and/or beneficial food
microorganisms and fermentation science
Must demonstrate competency in both oral and written communication, as well as interpersonal skills, to convey scientific information effectively to a diverse community.
Demonstrated ability to successfully design and implement innovative pedagogical methods in core and advanced courses and contribute to the development of the departmental curriculum, leading to demonstrable improvements in student learning outcomes and program assessment metrics.
Ability to successfully procure internal and external funding and publish the findings of their research in peer-reviewed, internationally recognized journals and through presentations.
Ability to collaborate externally with other institutions and stakeholders is expected to support
teaching, research, and service-related initiatives. Applicants must also be

Position Information
Position Overview
Essential Job Duties
The primary purpose of this position is to advance the major implementation, research, and program evaluation components of
Farm to Classroom: An immersive plant systems agriculture training program for teachers from the mountains to the sea.
This innovative initiative provides world-class professional development, including a transformative travel experience, to help selected North Carolina teachers integrate agriculture into their biology, environmental science, and other curricula. The ultimate goal will be a new model for teacher professional development that elevates students’ knowledge in food and agricultural sciences.
The successful candidate will conduct social sciences research in the initiative and serve in every component: teacher recruitment, professional development, communications, and program evaluation. Strong oral and written communication skills and a desire to extend skills in educational research and program evaluation are essential.
The postdoctoral research scholar will conduct innovative social sciences research, analyze qualitative and quantitative data sets, and disseminate findings through publications. The postdoctoral research scholar will maintain appropriate records regarding
Farm to Classroom: An immersive plant systems agriculture training program for teachers from the mountains to the sea
and competitive grant proposals. This career opportunity is suited to a motivated scholar with quantitative and qualitative social sciences research experience who seeks to advance extension and research with the guidance of accomplished supervisor(s) and mentor(s).
Duties and Responsibilities:
Analyze data, interpret findings, and contribute to the preparation of manuscripts and scholarly reports, including for Farm to Classroom: An immersive plant systems agriculture training program for teachers from the mountains to the sea.
Contribute to competitive grant proposals, especially in support of the Career Pathways Lab and NC State Extension Grow For It initiatives.
Collaborate in the coordination of research with mentor(s).
Coordinate multiple, concurrent assignments in extension, research, and program evaluation.
Maintain detailed research records, data sets, and methodological documentation.
Collaborate with graduate students, faculty, and project personnel to conduct programming, including participating in research meetings, progress updates, and planning.
Submit research and scholarly publications, in consultation with their mentor(s).
Complete all other duties as assigned.
The initial appointment is for two years with satisfactory performance, and the postdoctoral research scholar is expected to have ample opportunity to pursue extramural funding for this position to continue.

Primary Function of the Organizational Unit
North Carolina Cooperative Extension gives our residents easy access to resources and expertise of NC State University and NC A&T State University. Through educational programs, publications, and events, Cooperative Extension agents deliver unbiased, research-based information to North Carolina Citizens
Duties and Responsibilities
The Extension Administrative Assistant’s purpose is to provide program, clerical and bookkeeping support, to the Extension staff including the Extension Director and Extension Agents. These tasks are to be done requiring little to no supervision. Major duties include:
Provide administrative support, budget management, event planning, organizational communications and office administration.
Provide clerical support and program support for agents, such as Horticulture, Family and Consumer Sciences and 4-H Youth Development.
Performs office support duties of file management, purchasing and maintaining office supplies, reviewing/screening mail, processing outgoing mail.
Data entries on: Postage and travel.
Serves as Leave Coordinator for all staff members.

NCSU
Extension Policies.
This position must have knowledge of the total County Extension Program in order to assist all staff in continuation of educational programs during a position vacancy and assist the County Extension Director in training new staff members as positions are filled.
This position is responsible for receiving and obtaining pertinent information on all samples (plant soil, waste) which are brought into the office.
It serves as contact person between Extension office and county administration in all budgetary matters.
Attends County Department Head and
NCSU
Extension Admin. meetings as needed.
Assist Rutherford County Cattlemen’s Association.

This position is located in Lee County, NC.
Are you looking for a rewarding career that will let you give back to your community? NC State Extension extends research-based knowledge to all North Carolinians, helping them transform science into everyday solutions that improve their lives and grow our state. We are the model of excellence in non-formal, research-based education that advances agriculture, the environment, human health and well-being, youth and communities, while creating prosperity for all North Carolinians. Check out this
video
on what it’s like to work for NC State Extension.
While our employees are located in NC County offices, they reap the benefits of employment at NC State University. Some of our benefits include:
· Paid time off
· Paid tuition
· Health Insurance
· Flexible Scheduling
· Retirement Savings
· Supplemental Benefits Programs

You Belong Here! At NC State, our goal is for all employees to reach their fullest potential at work. As you consider this opportunity, we encourage you to review our
Employee Value Proposition

The Administrative Assistant is a member of the staff of the North Carolina Cooperative Extension (
NCCE
). The County Administrative Assistant’s primary responsibility is to provide support to the County Extension Director and other staff as assigned. The County Administrative Assistant cooperates with colleagues in the
NCCE
and county leaders in performing assigned responsibilities. The performance of assigned responsibility is governed by the philosophy, objectives, policies, and procedures of the
NCCE
.
Primary Function of Organizational Unit
North Carolina Cooperative Extension gives our residents easy access to the resources and expertise of NC State University and NC A&T State University. Through educational programs, publications, and events, NC Cooperative Extension Field Faculty deliver unbiased, research-based information to North Carolina citizens.
Duties and Responsibilities:
The County Administrative Assistant position provides office support and leadership, under supervision, to the Extension staff and does related work as required to support educational programs in a variety of areas including data entry, procurement and management of office supplies and programming materials and building schedule with room reservations.
Responsibilities include providing administrative support for the County Extension Director and other staff/program areas including Agriculture, Local Foods, 4-H, and Family and Consumer Science. Position interacts with other county departments (Human Resources, Finance, Budget, Infrastructure & Asset Management, etc.) to ensure effective relationships with county government and partner organizations working with Extension staff.
The County Administrative Assistant will work with the County Extension Director to develop and effectively manage the county budget and maintain monthly expense records.
Other Work/Responsibilities
The County Administrative Assistant will provide customer service to call-in and walk-in clientele and should have the ability to respond to all clientele. This position will be responsible for assisting in developing and maintaining county budgets, maintaining grant fund budgets, managing certain Extension reports and communicating with Extension administration, website updates, and creation of the monthly Extension newsletter.

Job Location

Department
University Budget Office
About the Department
The Division of Budget and Resource Management, an operating unit in the Office of Finance and Administration, is responsible for resource planning, financial and cost analysis, university budget preparation, monitoring, and control. The division’s departments provide university executive leadership with budget, financial, and cost analyses for operational and decision-making purposes.
The Budget Office, a Budget and Resource Management department, is responsible for the development, oversight, and reporting of the university’s operating budgets; budget administration, accounting, and cash management for capital improvement projects; development and maintenance of appropriated internal budget reporting; analysis of the multi-year financial plans, budgets, and recommended annual fee/charge increases of receipts supported organizations. In addition, the Budget Office also ensures that all financial and human resource systems development includes all budgetary and financial information required by the governing agencies. The Budget Office is also responsible for interfacing with external financial organizations such as
UNC
General Administration, Office of State Budget and Management, Legislative Fiscal Research, and financial officers of other institutions within the
UNC
System.

Essential Job Duties
The Sr. Budget Analysts conducts complex budget and financial analysis that informs recommendations to executive university leadership on resource allocation decisions and multi-year budget planning. Sr. Analysts meet with the
AVC
for Budget and Resource Management, Executive Vice Chancellor, Finance and Administration, and Executive Vice Chancellor and Provost to share analyses often used for making strategic decisions.
This role is designed for a seasoned budget professional who can translate complex financial data into clear, executive-level insight. Using tools such as SharePoint, Power BI, OneStream, and the PeopleSoft
ERP
systems (Financial, HR, and Student), you will develop reports and forecasts that support strategic decision-making. Additionally, you will provide consultative support to assigned units, overseeing revenues and expenditures across state-appropriated funds, appropriated receipts, F&A, student fees, and premium tuition. Success in this role requires strong analytical judgment, the ability to interpret complex policies and statutes, advanced data and visualization skills, and the collaboration and customer service mindset needed to support university leadership under tight deadlines.
Key Responsibilities fall into two primary functions outlined below:
Budget Management & Compliance:
Manage and implement the current year budget in compliance with state law and
UNC
System policies.
Advise colleges and units on budget balances, compliance, and position control.
Interpret statutes, session laws, and policies to guide assigned units.
Review and approve General Fund budget revisions and allotments.
Lead quarter-end and fiscal year-end closeout processes, including carryforward.
Perform budgeting and forecasting to ensure sufficient cash flow and reserve levels.
Conduct periodic budget reviews to identify risks and develop proactive solutions.
Strategic Analysis & Executive Reporting:
Provide budget analysis and recommendations that support executive decision-making and long-term resource allocation.
Advise leadership on university reserve allocation and maintain accurate reconciliation schedules.
Query, transform, and analyze complex data sets to produce dashboards and business intelligence reports.
Complete ad-hoc financial analyses for leadership, system offices, and legislative requests.
Prepare and analyze the Base Budget as part of the General Fund Biennial Budget development process.
If you are a detail-oriented financial professional who thrives on transforming complex data into strategic insight, values collaboration, and brings both technical expertise and sound judgment to high-impact budget decisions, we encourage you to apply.
Other Responsibilities
In addition to the core responsibilities of this role, there may be occasional opportunities to take on additional duties. This offers an opportunity to build on your skills, explore new areas, and gain a deeper understanding of the department’s operations.
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Requires post-baccalaureate credentials plus 1-2 years experience; a bachelor’s degree and 2-4 years experience may be substituted for the advanced degree on an exceptional basis.
Other Required Qualifications
Post-baccalaureate credentials or a bachelor’s degree in business, finance, accounting, computer science, information technology, or a related field.
Demonstrated capacity to research, interpret, and apply state statutes, policies, and regulations, including the State Budget Act and
UNC
policy guidance.
Advanced proficiency with Excel and financial systems, including creation of pivot tables, database queries, and data visualizations (e.g., Power BI or similar tools).
Knowledge of monitoring state appropriations, appropriated receipts, and F&A funds to ensure compliance with applicable policies and regulations.
Proven skill in managing multiple priorities, meeting tight deadlines, and working independently or leading collaborative efforts.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills with a demonstrated customer service orientation and commitment to continuous process improvement.
Preferred Qualifications
Prior budgeting experience in a University setting;
Significant knowledge of and experience with budgets and accounting with the ability to exercise financial strategy in managing multiple funding sources and types;
Knowledge of accounting to create and review budgets and actuals journals in the financial system;
Demonstrated experience managing customer relationships, improving customer satisfaction, and resolving difficult customer issues.
Required License(s) or Certification(s)
N/A

Yes

No

About the Department
The Department of Chemistry at NC State University provides professional education for chemists at the undergraduate and graduate levels; teaches introductory chemistry courses, which are needed by students enrolled in other department programs; advances the discipline of chemistry by conducting research; serves the public by sharing information about chemistry through a variety of mechanisms and services; and commits to creating environments that are in alignment with our mission, vision and values. Our department is home to award-winning faculty and is one of NC State’s top producers of patent and invention disclosures. More information about the department can be found at
https://chemistry.sciences.ncsu.edu/
. While working in this department, you will be expected to foster a supportive and welcoming environment.
NC State promotes an integrated approach to problem solving that transforms lives and provides leadership for social, economic, and technological development across North Carolina and around the world. NC State’s land grant mission of teaching, research and service is dedicated to the service of North Carolina and its people. Applicants are encouraged to review the institution’s mission, vision and strategic plan, and consider how their background, interest and experience would enable them to support the university.

Essential Job Duties
Are you a highly organized, service-oriented professional who enjoys working with students, faculty, and data in a dynamic academic environment? The Department of Chemistry is seeking an Undergraduate Program Coordinator (
UPC
) to serve as the operational and administrative leader of our Undergraduate Program Office.
About the Role
The
UPC
plays a central role in shaping the undergraduate experience. As the primary support partner to the Co-Directors of Undergraduate Programs (Co-DUPs), you will serve as the first point of contact for students, parents, faculty, and staff on program policies, procedures, and academic matters. You will be a trusted resource, problem-solver, and advisor—ensuring students receive accurate guidance and timely support throughout their academic journey.
Key Responsibilities:
Student & Faculty Support
Advise and assist Chemistry majors and non-majors on program policies and processes
Support transcript reviews, transfer credit evaluations, and Non-Degree Seeking student enrollment
Process Plan/Subplan forms and assist with CourseLeaf actions
Review degree audits, clear seniors for graduation, and approve graduates in
SIS
Resolve record discrepancies in coordination with university administrative offices
Supervise temporary staff within the Undergraduate Office
Program Operations & Events
Coordinate all aspects of Fall and Spring graduation ceremonies
Manage the senior awards process and support award selection
Serve as liaison to Undergraduate Admissions,
COS
Academic Affairs, Registration and Records, and other university partners
Scheduling & Systems Management
Serve as the department’s primary scheduling officer
Build and manage the semester course schedule for undergraduate and graduate offerings
Assign instructors, coordinate classroom space, and submit
ASTRA
requests
Enter and maintain all scheduling data in the Student Information System (
SIS
)
Data Analysis & Reporting
Analyze course offerings, enrollment trends, instructor availability, credit hour production, and space utilization to support strategic planning
Prepare reports for departmental and university leadership
Compile data for accreditation and reporting requirements, including
SACS
,
ACS
(annual and five-year reports), the Southeast Chemistry Chairs Conference, and the Department Annual Report
Communications & Alumni Engagement
Serve on the department’s Communications Committee
Manage and maintain undergraduate webpages and digital content
Publish news posts and write four annual feature articles highlighting undergraduate students or alumni
Oversee Chemistry Alumni Relations in partnership with the Office of Advancement
Maintain the alumni database, coordinate communications, and organize alumni events
Outreach Support
Assist with faculty outreach efforts to schools, scientific institutions, and industry partners
The
UPC
is essential to ensuring a seamless, supportive, and data-informed undergraduate program. This position combines student engagement, academic administration, event coordination, strategic reporting, and digital communications—offering a unique opportunity to make a meaningful impact across the department.
Other Responsibilities
Other tasks and responsibilities may be assigned based on the needs of the organization and evolving priorities.
Qualifications
Minimum Experience/Education
Bachelor’s degree; or an equivalent combination of training and experience.
All degrees must be received from appropriately accredited institutions.
Preferred Qualifications
Previous university/academic experience or education-related environment with focus on student success.
Experience with NC State Student Information System (
SIS
) and GoogleApps Suite & Calendar/Gmail.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with proven ability to provide support to internal and external stakeholders.
Ability to maintain attention to detail, stay organized and multitask in a fast-paced environment.

About the Department
The Division of Academic and Student Affairs (
DASA
) is where students discover connections and community. It is where students live, where they gather, where they improve themselves, each other, and their university.
DASA
is where students seek guidance and support, both in and outside of the classroom. The division serves all students at NC State through three integrated components: academic affairs, student affairs, and University College.
NC State wants employees to achieve their highest potential at work, learn more about our employee value proposition here (
You Belong Here
). The Division of Academic and Student Affairs is the place to work for values-driven faculty and staff who are passionate about promoting the success of the whole student.
Academic Success Center
By hiring and training qualified students to provide a variety of academic services, the Academic Success Center (
ASC
) promotes students to become independent learners and therefore contribute to NC State undergraduate and graduate students’ academic success. The
ASC
also interacts with on and off-campus partners to share expertise about the tutoring field.

Essential Job Duties
The Associate Director is a full-time, 12-month, non-tenure track staff (
EPS
) position. As a key member of the Academic Success Center (
ASC
) team, the Associate Director has responsibility for assisting the Director by taking an active role in all major functions of the
ASC
, including teaching, training, planning, assessing, and promoting its programs.
Specific duties include, but are not limited to:
Administration (30%)
Independent administrative oversight of the Supplemental Instruction (SI) and Academic Peer Mentoring programs, including hiring, scheduling, and providing training for all SI Leaders, SI Peer Supervisors, and Academic Peer Mentors
Maintain communication with faculty regarding the SI program’s mission
Provide leadership for maintaining the unit’s website and communication channels
Lead the efforts for the recruiting and hiring of new
ASC
tutors
Work with the Director and the rest of the
ASC
team to formulate and implement
ASC
policies and procedures.
Assessment (25%)
Assess tutoring results by maintaining data entry and collection procedures, analyzing and reporting data to various groups on a regular basis
Serve as primary contact between the unit and the
DASA
Assessment office, ensuring completion of the unit’s assessment plan
Teaching (20%)
Teach one-credit tutor-training course each semester
Teach one-credit training course for new academic peer mentors
Supervision (15%)
Supervise 2 professional staff members
Support the Coordinator in oversight of the Group and Drop-in Tutoring programs
Supervise student staff and peer supervisors who work in both the SI and Academic Peer Mentoring programs
Programming (10%)
Facilitate workshops, classroom visits, and other resources to promote services to campus partners and students
Represent the unit to outside constituents at the Director’s request
Other Responsibilities
Other tasks and responsibilities may be assigned based on the needs of the organization and evolving priorities.
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Requires a relevant post-Baccalaureate degree with a minimum of three (3) years or greater of related professional experience.
Other Required Qualifications
Extensive undergraduate coursework in the physical and mathematical sciences.
Demonstrated experience in tutor training and/or assessing academic support programs.
Experience with web design and/or programming.
Preferred Qualifications
Masters degree in mathematics education, science education, instructional administration, student development or related field.
Teaching and/or administrative experience at the college level.

Essential Job Duties
Serve as the Insurance Coordinator for the Chester Grant Sports Medicine Center in Reynolds Coliseum, supporting the delivery of medical, mental health, and performance services to student-athletes through insurance coordination, medical documentation management, scheduling, and administrative support.
Coordinate insurance verification and assist with precertification processes for student-athletes.
Schedule onsite and offsite physician and specialist appointments.
Assist with maintaining and managing medical records, transcribing dictated notes, securing follow-up documentation from medical visits, and entering records into tracking databases for over 600 student-athletes.
Serve as a point of contact for student-athletes, providers, staff, and campus partners while ensuring compliance with
HIPAA
and departmental guidelines.
Manage administrative functions including calendar oversight, purchase orders, travel coordination, invoice processing, and purchase card reconciliation for Sports Medicine and Mental Health & Performance staff.
Assist student-athletes, physicians, nutritionists, mental health providers, administrators, and coaches (when appropriate) in support of care delivery.
This position is open only for current, permanent NC State Employees.

Other duties as assigned by Insurance Coordinator and Assistant Athletic Director for Sports Medicine and Administration.

Demonstrated knowledge of
HIPAA
regulations and experience handling protected health information in a compliant setting.
Working knowledge of medical terminology and common sports-related injuries and procedures.
Experience managing precertification processes, insurance coordination, and follow-up documentation for medical visits.
Proven ability to interpret written and verbal policy information, manage multiple priorities, and maintain accurate records in a fast-paced administrative setting.

Essential Job Duties
The Extension Specialist – Quality & Management Systems delivers high-impact technical assistance, training, and program leadership focused on quality and management systems. The role blends technical consulting, facilitation, project management, and service development to support manufacturers and organizations across North Carolina.
The Specialist independently leads client engagements, facilitates public and on-site courses, and contributes to the design, delivery, and expansion of
IES
quality-focused services. This position requires strong technical expertise, facilitation skills, leadership capability, and the ability to balance client service with program operations and impact reporting.
Core Competencies & Responsibilities
1. Facilitation & Client Engagement
Provide hands-on technical assistance, coaching, and facilitation to organizations implementing, maintaining, or improving quality and management systems, including
ISO
9001,
IATF
16949, and related standards. Lead and support activities such as system implementation, internal auditing, corrective action, and sustainment, while interpreting standards and tailoring practical, sustainable, client-owned solutions. Facilitate effective face-to-face and virtual engagements—including public courses, on-site training, workshops, and client meetings—using adult learning best practices. Assess client needs, guide organizations through improvement pathways, present content clearly across formal and informal learning environments, and build trust with varied audiences by adapting facilitation approaches to varying skill levels and organizational cultures.
2. Program & Thought Leadership
Provide support for one or more quality-focused products, services, or initiatives within
IES
. Maintain awareness of
IES
offerings, industry trends, and best practices to align services with client and statewide needs. Contribute to service development, continuous improvement of offerings, and collaboration across
IES
teams to strengthen program impact.
3. Business Development & Outreach
Promote the full range of
IES
services through client interactions, on-site visits, partner networking, and presentations. Identify opportunities to connect clients with additional
IES
resources that support long-term improvement and organizational success. Represent NC State University professionally with clients, partners, and stakeholders.
4. Program Operations & Impact Reporting
Manage administrative and operational responsibilities that support effective service delivery, including project scoping, scheduling, Salesforce updates, travel documentation, and required reporting. Support data collection and evaluation processes used to measure and communicate the economic and operational impact of
IES
programs to state, federal, and local stakeholders.
Hybrid working options may be available.
Other Responsibilities
Support outreach, promotion, and cross-selling of
IES
services.
Contribute to continuous improvement of training materials and service offerings.
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Bachelor’s degree with relevant professional experience.
Strong working knowledge of
ISO
9001 with hands-on implementation experience.
Other Required Qualifications
Independent client engagement leadership
Coaching and facilitation
Project management
Quality management systems expertise
Preferred Qualifications
IATF
16949 experience strongly preferred

,

,

Experience with

About the Department
The Division of Academic and Student Affairs (
DASA
) is where students discover connections and community. It is where students live, where they gather, where they improve themselves, each other, and their university.
DASA
is where students seek guidance and support, both in and outside of the classroom. The division serves all students at NC State through three integrated components: academic affairs, student affairs, and University College.
NC State wants employees to achieve their highest potential at work, learn more about our employee value proposition here (
You Belong Here
). The Division of Academic and Student Affairs is the place to work for values-driven faculty and staff who are passionate about promoting the success of the whole student.
We promote the success of the whole student. In collaboration with our colleagues across campus, we prepare students to succeed academically, professionally and personally, to embrace a commitment to lifelong learning, and to become informed, engaged, and productive citizens.
The purpose of Campus Health Services is to provide appropriate, affordable, accessible and high quality primary health care to eligible members of the campus community including students, staff and faculty, in a compassionate and caring manner. The major function of the clinic is that of providing outpatient care to the 34,000+ NC State students and limited services to faculty and staff.
All of our staff members have standards that guide the work we do every day: Campus Health Values are Respect, Integrity, Customer Service, Teamwork, and Excellence.

Essential Job Duties
This position provides technical assistance to the Radiology Department at Campus Health. The individual serving in this position will act as the patient care and diagnostic technician for the department, providing internal customer service, technical assistance, data collection, and patient care. The position also requires cross training in phlebotomy (training provided) to assist
laboratory in high demand times.
Primary job duties:
Interact with internal staff, patients, and referring providers through e-mail, phone, and personal communications.
Keep equipment clean and in working order.
Responsible for maintaining equipment and quality control.
Attend scheduled meetings, drills and training.
Perform all other duties as assigned.
Perform technical procedures including patient positioning for radiographs and digital image capturing.
Provide excellent patient care by applying full knowledge of anatomy, physiology, and disease processes.
Provide quick and accurate information regarding each patient, acting as a liaison between clinicians and radiologists.
Be proficient in entering data into the electronic health record.
Maintain a clean working environment to ensure Infectious Diseases standards are followed and infection is not spread.
Handle medical/biological and infectious waste appropriately.
Follow all radiation safety,
OSHA
, and Environmental Health and Safety standards.
Key Qualifications:
Experience with Electronic Health Records software
Skilled in the use of radiology equipment and in the performance of procedures
Other Responsibilities
Participate in fire and emergency preparedness drills.
Must report to work during Adverse Weather and related scenarios and for public health emergencies.
Qualifications
Minimum Experience/Education
High school diploma or equivalency and two years of experience in medical diagnostics; or an equivalent combination of training and experience.
Some positions may require additional training, certifications, or licenses.
Preferred Qualifications
General knowledge of the basic pathophysiological process being monitored and how radiologic science is applied.
Ability to work independently and practice good judgement to ensure high quality care.
Ability to perform specialized orthopedic procedures such as weight bearing lower extremities.
Ability to perform or learn phlebotomy for high volume days.

ARRT
certification
CPR
certification

Essential Job Duties
To assist in the day-to-day operation of the NC State University Plant Disease and Insect Clinic. Specific duties will include logging incoming samples into the clinic database application, performing diagnostic assays (plating,
PPP
; immunostrip, incubation, soil pH/EC determination, etc.) on plant samples as directed by diagnosticians, preparing agar media, replenishing supplies as used, washing glassware and plastic boxes, assisting with updating of the supplies inventory, and other duties as assigned. These positions will also share responsibilities for recording payments received and preparing checks for deposit with the
CALS
Business Office.
Work Schedule:
The schedule for the position varies according to the workload in the Plant Disease and Insect Clinic. The anticipated workload is 15-19 hours per week May through August, about 10 hours per week in September, October, March, and April, and 5-10 hours per week November through February.

PG194496EP

No
Working Title
Director for Special Projects, Planning & Strategic Initiatives
Anticipated Hiring Range
Commensurate upon experience
Work Schedule
Monday - Friday, 8 am - 5 pm
Job Location
Raleigh, NC
Department
Lampe Joint Department of Biomedical Engineering
About the Department
The mission of the Lampe Joint Department of Biomedical Engineering is to provide leadership through exceptional educational programs and experiences that prepare engineers and scientists to meet the challenges of biomedical discovery and applications of engineering to medicine. The research and entrepreneurship of the faculty, students, and staff aim to accumulate, generate, and disseminate biomedical engineering knowledge to improve health care for the benefit of the people of North Carolina, the nation, and the world.
Wolfpack Perks and Benefits
As a Pack member,
you belong here,
and can enjoy
exclusive perks
designed to enhance your personal and professional well-being. As you consider this opportunity, we encourage you to review our
Employee Value Proposition
and learn more about what makes NC State the best place to learn and work for everyone.

and

Childcare
benefits,
Wellness & Recreation Membership
, and
Wellness Programs
that aim to build a thriving wolfpack community.
Disclaimer: Perks and Benefit eligibility is based on Part-Time or Full-Time Employment status. Eligibility and Employer Sponsored Plans can be found within each of the links offered.
Essential Job Duties
The Director for Special Projects, Planning & Strategic Initiatives is a critical, high-impact leadership role, reporting directly to the Department Head/Chair of the Lampe Joint Department of Biomedical Engineering (
BME
).
This position is responsible for overseeing the strategic planning, structural integrity, and successful execution of high-priority initiatives unique to the BME’s dual-campus environment (NC State and
UNC
-Chapel Hill). The Director will serve as the Department Head’s executive project manager, translating the departmental vision into actionable goals, ensuring the unique administrative functions support core academic and research missions, and driving strategic projects to completion. This role is expected to work closely with the College of Engineering at NC State, the College of Arts and Sciences, and the School of Medicine at
UNC
Chapel Hill Operations Offices to ensure departmental administrative processes are aligned with broader College and School policies and to streamline the resolution of complex, cross-institutional issues. This role is a key steward of the unique inter-institutional knowledge required to maintain the department’s operation.
Key Responsibilities
Departmental Strategic Planning and Implementation (45%)
Strategic Execution
: Lead the formulation, implementation, and tracking of the department’s strategic plan and high-priority initiatives, ensuring alignment with the long-term goals of
BME
, NC State’s College of Engineering, and
UNC
-CH’s School of Medicine/College of Arts and Sciences.
Project Leadership:
Manages and oversees all special projects assigned by the Department Head/Chair. Projects include, but are not limited to, maximizing the strategic impact and compliance of newly established endowments; leading major facility and space‑utilization projects across both campuses; contributing to the development of new educational or research programs; and participating in hiring committees for development and communications positions.
Decision Support:
Work with department leadership team and provide strategic decision support to the Department Head/Chair by conducting research, analyzing internal trends (e.g., enrollment, research funding, dual-campus personnel data), benchmarking against peer institutions, and preparing high-level slide decks, reports, letters and presentations to aid in informed decision-making. This includes anticipating threats and opportunities related to BME’s growth and financial stability.
Structural Stewardship, Operations, and Administrative Efficiency (35%)
BME
Charter Stewardship
: Serve as the definitive steward of the Lampe Joint
BME
Charter and its associated appendix agreements, which form the unique foundation for a single department administered by two universities and three colleges/schools. This involves assisting in enforcing the Charter’s terms with administrative units at both NC State and
UNC
-CH and assisting in drafting and executing necessary updates to the agreements.
Operational Optimization
: Streamline and optimize the department’s administrative workflows, improve internal communication structures across both campuses, and manage the administrative calendar to ensure key inter-institutional deadlines (e.g., HR, budget, grant submissions) are met efficiently.
Policy Implementation & Issue Resolution
: Serve as the department’s expert on complex institutional policies for both NC State and
UNC
-Chapel Hill and ensure consistent, accurate application of these policies across all departmental operations. This role proactively resolves issues as they arise and implements necessary policy changes to maintain the department’s unique functioning.
College Alignment and Strategic Stakeholder Engagement (20%)
Inter-Institutional Advocacy
: Serve as the primary operational liaison and advocate for
BME
with leadership at both NC State and
UNC
-Chapel Hill to maintain the department’s special processes required to pursue its endowed mission.
Development and Communications Support:
Collaborate closely with the College/School Directors of Development and Communications to prepare strategic vision materials, coordinate external engagement, and support outreach to alumni, industry, and friends for philanthropic, collaborative, and research efforts.
External Coordination:
Coordinate efforts across varied internal and external stakeholders—including faculty at both universities, administrative teams, physician groups, and industry partners—to implement key strategic programs and projects, leveraging the department’s unique dual-campus structure.
Other Responsibilities
Other tasks and responsibilities may be assigned based on the needs of the organization and evolving priorities.
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
A master’s or advanced degree in a relevant field such as business administration, public administration; or a related discipline with at least 3 years of experience.
Other Required Qualifications
Demonstrated experience in a high-level project management or executive operations role, preferably within a complex academic or research environment that requires navigating multiple institutional policies or systems.
Proven ability to manage multiple large-scale initiatives simultaneously and ensure deadlines are met.
Excellent communication skills (written and verbal), with a demonstrated ability to serve as a central point of contact and advocate for a specialized unit among varied, high-level stakeholders.
Experience in optimizing workflows, improving communication structures, and fostering a positive work environment.
Demonstrated ability to provide strategic decision support through research, data analysis, and insightful presentation.
Preferred Qualifications
Knowledge or direct experience managing a joint or inter-institutional administrative unit, proven leadership skills with experience in supervising and leading a team.
Required License(s) or Certification(s)
N/A

No
Commercial Driver's License required
No

Essential Job Duties
Candidate will be responsible for conducting field and laboratory research through the NC State Apiculture Program. This position will support several ongoing research projects, including but not limited to reproductive physiology of honey bee males (drones), field collections of native bee communities, and molecular analyses of bee diseases. Candidate will be responsible for data collection, sample preparation, data entry, and coordination among other team members. The position will also entail some basic beekeeping support with the Apiculture Technician.
Work Schedule:
The normal work schedule will be five (5) days per week for eight (8) hours per day. Flexibility in schedule, however, is necessary for the position. Many research projects, for example, necessitate longer hours for a given day or working on weekends.

About the Department
The Program Manager will be an employee in the Department of Biological and Agricultural Engineering (
BAE
) jointly administered by the College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (
CALS
) and the College of Engineering at NC State University. The department actively contributes to the academic, research and extension missions of the University.
Wolfpack Perks and Benefits

Essential Job Duties
We are seeking a highly organized, collaborative, and forward-thinking Program Manager to lead coordination for the
REFRAME
Project
—a four-year, externally funded research initiative developing a modular, AI-enabled, open-source platform for feedstock-agnostic evaluation of future biomass systems.
REFRAME
integrates legacy open-source models, novel surrogate models, shared metadata frameworks, and advanced large language model (
LLM
) tools to make biomass systems analysis more accessible, transparent, and impactful for researchers, industry leaders, and policymakers.
This is a unique opportunity to contribute to a high-visibility, interdisciplinary effort at the intersection of AI, sustainability, and digital infrastructure.
Why This Role Matters
As Program Manager, you will serve as an operational support of REFRAME—ensuring the project advances on schedule, within budget, and in alignment with sponsor requirements and institutional policies. You’ll act as the central hub connecting faculty, researchers, administrators, and external partners across multiple institutions.
The
REFRAME
team is a dynamic and growing collaboration of:
11 faculty and senior researchers
7 graduate students and postdoctoral scholars
5 administrative support staff
Multiple academic, industry, and stakeholder partners
What You’ll Do
Program & Project Management (45%)
Develop and manage integrated project plans, including milestones, deliverables, dependencies, and key decision points
Coordinate activities across three program pillars: modeling, digital infrastructure, and stakeholder engagement
Oversee budgeting, procurement, subcontracting, and compliance in partnership with university administrative units
Support long-range strategy, scope adjustments, and prioritization as project needs evolve
Communication & Collaboration (35%)
Serve as the primary coordination point among partner institutions and research teams
Plan and facilitate recurring meetings, leadership briefings, workshops, and site visits
Prepare agendas, decision summaries, and action tracking to ensure clarity and accountability
Maintain structured documentation and consistent communication across a distributed collaboration
Oversee updates to the
REFRAME
website, ensuring alignment with funder and program messaging
Reporting, Documentation & Data Governance (20%)
Track performance metrics across program pillars
Prepare progress reports, sponsor updates, and technical documentation
Maintain shared repositories, project records, and data governance documentation
Support open-source release coordination and repository management
Ensure compliance with sponsor and institutional reporting requirements
What Makes This Opportunity Special
Be part of a nationally significant, AI-enabled sustainability initiative
Work at the intersection of digital infrastructure, modeling, and stakeholder engagement
Collaborate with leading researchers and cross-sector partners
Contribute to open-source tools designed for broad, long-term impact
Play a foundational role in shaping a growing, interdisciplinary program
You Belong Here! At NC State, our goal is for all employees to reach their fullest potential at work. As you consider this opportunity, we encourage you to review our
Employee Value Proposition
(
https://news.hr.ncsu.edu/you-belong-here/
) and learn more about what makes NC State the best place to learn and work for everyone.
Other Responsibilities
Other tasks and responsibilities may be assigned based on the needs of the organization and evolving priorities.
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Bachelor’s degree and 3–5 years of project or program management experience, or an equivalent combination of education and relevant professional experience in scientific, engineering, technical, or research environments.
Other Required Qualifications
Excellent organizational, written, and interpersonal communication skills.
Ability to facilitate decision-making among varied teams and leadership groups.
Ability to manage multiple priorities, timelines, and stakeholder needs with minimal supervision.
Preferred Qualifications
5+ years of experience in project or program management.
Experience coordinating large, multi-institutional or multi-partner initiatives (e.g., academic–industry–government collaborations).
Experience supporting federally or philanthropically funded research projects, including reporting and compliance requirements.
Familiarity with scientific or technical project workflows, data governance, or open-source software development practices.
Proficiency with project management and collaboration tools (e.g., GitHub, Google Workspace, Zoom, shared dashboards, task tracking).
Required License(s) or Certification(s)
Valid driver’s license.
Valid NC driver’s license within 60 days of hire.

About the Department
The Division of Academic & Student Affairs (
DASA
) is where students discover connections and community. It is where students live, where they gather, where they improve themselves, each other, and their university.
DASA
is where students seek guidance and support, both in and outside of the classroom. The division serves all students at NC State through three integrated components: academic affairs, student affairs, and University College.
NC State wants employees to achieve their highest potential at work, learn more about our employee value proposition here (
You Belong Here
). The Division of Academic and Student Affairs is the place to work for values-driven faculty and staff who are passionate about promoting the success of the whole student.
NC State Prevention Services provides case management, education and outreach emphasizing holistic, developmental, and non-judgmental perspectives for student prevention efforts. Through the lenses of behavioral health, wellness, and sustainability, our professionals cross-collaborate with the campus community to enhance individual and collective well-being, as well as assisting students to gain the necessary resources to remain successful academically and emotionally at NC State.

Essential Job Duties
The Associate Director is a member of the Leadership Team for Prevention Services and will serve as a resource and consultant to all staff and the campus community. The Associate Director is required to perform administrative duties including assessment, offer leadership in psychoeducation, suicide prevention, and general outreach, provide direct service to students, consult with university faculty/staff, and participate in supervision and training. This person will assist in developing, revising, and maintaining policies and procedures to ensure adherence to best practices and legal and ethical codes. This person will be responsible for the coordination of teams and committees and will directly offer administrative supervision for permanent staff members, graduate assistants, or temporary or student workers. There is an expectation that Directors will assist in hiring, training, supervising, and consulting with all staff, including trainees, as needed.
This staff member serves on the Administrative Team, along with the current Directors in the department. The Associate Director will supervise Prevention Services operations in the absence of the Director. Members of the Administrative Team will be expected to be available for after-hours postvention support, submit reports and/or data to the Director as requested and will serve on committees within and outside of the Center as required.
General Duties and Responsibilities:
Training & Outreach Administration
Oversee suicide prevention and outreach program including developing and offering comprehensive outreach and consultation services to faculty, staff, students, and families via in-person and multimedia presentations.
Coordinate and deliver training in
QPR
(Question, Persuade, & Refer Suicide Prevention) and other prevention programs to the campus community.
Oversee the coordination of
AOD
and Recovery, including grant administration.
Coordinate the completion of the
DAAPP
(Drug and Alcohol-Free Schools) and Biennial Report for alcohol prevention and education
Assist in development of outreach emphasis positions based on training and content area needs.
Supervise Mental Health Ambassador program.
Maintain contracts with Impact,
BASICS
, eCheckup, Vector, or other
AOD
and Outreach platforms used by Prevention Services, including IT and budget oversight.
Coordinate on-going postvention needs in collaboration with the Counseling Center.
Participate in the after-hours postvention and case review rotation
Serve as primary back-up for outreach events needing coverage (occasional evening and weekend hours required for outreach).
Direct Student Support & Case Management
Conduct behaviorally and clinically informed one-on-one assessments to identify and mitigate barriers to student success, including self-harm and threat assessment.
Provide timely and appropriate referrals to internal and external support services.
Coordinate intervention plans with university and off-campus resources to resolve student concerns.
Monitor and track individual cases and student progress to ensure compliance with recommendations and address emerging issues.
Manage re-entry strategies for students returning from psychological hospitalization.
Administration & Supervision
Supervise staff in documenting student concerns and action items accurately in the Maxient and Impact.
Ethically assess data collected about students of concern to develop and maintain best practices.
This position will directly supervise 3-5 permanent staff members.
Engage with and supervise interns, graduate assistants, and temporary employees.
Maintain current licensure through CEs and attend additional trainings offered by the university
Assist in assessment and report writing for the department.
Oversee office operations when the Director is not available.
Perform other duties within the scope of a prevention specialist, as assigned.
Other Responsibilities
Other duties as assigned
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Master’s degree in Social Work, Psychology, or Counseling
Hold current full clinical licensure in NC or able to obtain this licensure status within six months of hire
Minimum of five years of post-licensure experience
Other Required Qualifications
Excellent interpersonal skills including both oral and written communication in a variety of settings
Demonstrated ability to identify problems and use sound judgement and reasoning to make crucial decisions autonomously
Demonstrated experience in providing individualized support for students in crisis or who have been identified as exhibiting concerning or worrisome behaviors
Ability to establish collaborative relationships with campus and community partners
Preferred Qualifications
Demonstrated knowledge of case management systems (preferably in a higher education setting), and/or experience working as part of a case management support team
Demonstrated history of effective collaborations across a complex array of multiple agencies within a university setting
Strong working knowledge of various federal and state laws affecting issues of privacy and confidentiality, disability accommodations and support, and response to sexual violence
Experience with supervision of staff and training
Broad knowledge of best practices and current national trends related to higher education case management
Experience with grants and report writing
Required License(s) or Certification(s)
Independently licensed in a mental health field

About the Department
The overall goals of the Center for Applied Aquatic Ecology (
CAAE
) are to provide water quality information to optimize management of water resources and to provide opportunities for students and teachers through education/outreach programs. Our work requires various facilities and equipment, such as a state-certified water quality analytical laboratory and real-time remote monitoring field instrumentation. High-quality data from the
CAAE
contributes to the State environmental agency’s water quality database and to
NCSU
students’ research projects.
CAAE
water quality monitoring/research programs help safeguard potable source-water reservoirs depended upon by approximately 750,000 North Carolinians.
CAAE
programs also track water quality in selected estuaries and marine coastal areas.

Essential Job Duties
Join our team at the CAAE’s state-certified laboratory, where your work will directly support water quality research and meaningful community education initiatives.
We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated
Research Technician
to perform water quality analyses using sophisticated instrumentation and established analytical techniques. This is an exciting opportunity to contribute to environmental research while also engaging with students, educators, and community groups through our outreach programs.
What You’ll Do
In this role, you will:
Conduct comprehensive water quality analyses, including nutrients (nitrogen, phosphorus, nitrate, ammonia/ammonium, total Kjeldahl nitrogen, soluble reactive phosphorus), suspended solids, chlorophyll
a
, and fecal bacteria in accordance with the
CAAE
Standard Operating Procedures.
Operate and maintain advanced laboratory instrumentation, including auto-analyzers and specialized research equipment.
Prepare reagents, calculate chemical quantities, and ensure precise application for analytical procedures.
Monitor data quality, identify discrepancies, and organize and prepare datasets for analysis and reporting.
Provide preliminary summaries of laboratory procedures, tests, and research findings.
Perform routine maintenance and minor repairs on laboratory equipment to ensure optimal performance.
Maintain an organized, safe, and compliant laboratory environment in alignment with QA/QC, sanitation, and safety protocols.
Manage laboratory inventory, track supply usage, and coordinate ordering of materials for analytical and bacterial testing.
Collaborate with special projects, including nutrient analysis and coordinated research efforts with ancillary units.
Independently prepare and maintain bench layouts to support efficient laboratory workflow.
Education & Outreach Engagement
Beyond the lab, you’ll have the opportunity to make science accessible and inspiring by:
Assisting with the CAAE’s education and outreach initiatives.
Hosting visiting groups for laboratory tours and hands-on activities.
Supporting laboratory and field-based educational experiences.
Coordinating and utilizing interactive tools such as the Augmented Reality Sandbox.
Traveling occasionally to off-campus locations for scientific and educational activities.
What We’re Looking For
Strong technical skills in laboratory analysis and instrumentation.
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
Excellent attention to detail and commitment to quality assurance and safety standards.
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple responsibilities.
Enthusiasm for engaging with students and community audiences in an educational setting.
Other Responsibilities
Other tasks and responsibilities may be assigned based on the needs of the organization and evolving priorities.
Qualifications
Minimum Experience/Education
Demonstrated possession of the competencies necessary to perform the work.
Preferred Qualifications
Must be willing to travel to collect water samples, and for scientific and educational activities.
Must have excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail and record-keeping.
Working knowledge of Quality Assurance/Quality Control and Standard Operating Procedure documents.
Proficiency in Excel.
Experience in an analytical laboratory setting.
Required License or Certification
Valid Driver’s License and the ability to obtain and NC Driver’s License within 60 days of hire.
Valid NC Driver's License required
Yes

No

About the Department
Butner Beef Cattle Field Lab (
BBCFL
) is a 1,250 acre research unit located about 50 miles north of campus with a staff of 9.5
FTE
.
BBCFL
serves as a research platform for research on beef cattle including nutrition, physiology, genetics, pest control, pasture management, crop production and related areas.
BBCFL
serves as a training and demonstration area utilized by graduate student and the general public.

Essential Job Duties
This position serves as the manager of the
BBCFL
Cow/Calf Division and oversees an expanding herd that currently has approximately 600 head of cattle, including cows, calves, replacement heifers, and bulls. The position will direct the daily activities of 1 – 3 employees, including research technicians, temporary employees, and student employees at any given time.
Key Responsibilities:
Research project coordination and data collection
: Collaborates with Principal Investigators (PIs) on research design and pasture utilization. Collects biological samples (blood, rumen fluid), forage, and weights while strictly adhering to research protocols
Reproduction management
: Performs advanced reproductive tasks including artificial insemination (AI), estrus synchronization, and pregnancy determinations.
Herd Health Management
: Independently monitors nutrition and health. Collaborates with the Vet School to develop and execute vaccination and disease prevention strategies. Mixing and administering vaccines and medications.
Calving & Neonatal Care:
Oversees the calving process, provides manual assistance (pulling calves) when necessary, and manages all calf processing.
Records & Software Management
: Maintains accurate cattle registrations via management software and the American Angus Association. Summarizes data for research reports and maintains monthly inventory
Facility & Equipment Oversight:
Ensures all equipment (scales, AI tools, auto-syringes) is calibrated and repaired. Manages inventory of medicinals and supplies while ensuring all staff follow strict safety protocols
Other Responsibilities
Assists with general grounds, fence, building, and equipment maintenance for all of
BBCFL
. Duties include, but are not limited to, mowing, weed eating, power washing, scraping manure, and general housekeeping.
Assists with hay production and corn silage harvest.
Assists other units at
BBCFL
as needed.
Qualifications
Minimum Experience/Education
Demonstrated possession of the competencies necessary to perform the work.
Preferred Qualifications
Experience in agricultural work, specifically related to beef cattle production and animal care
Ability to perform artificial insemination and assist with difficult births
Experience performing routine cattle herd health procedures.
Experience with cattle software programs
Experience in general agricultural equipment operation
Required License or Certification
Valid Driver’s License
Pesticide Applicators License, or the ability to obtain within 6 months
Beef Quality Assurance Certification, or the ability to obtain within 6 months

Yes

About the Department
As part of the Land Grant University, the Department of Entomology and Plant Pathology is composed of over 40 faculty that direct a varied portfolio of programs in teaching, research and extension components. Collectively, these strengths not only serve our clientele and our science but also provide a rich environment for graduate education and training that can be tailored to the specific career interests of the student, irrespective of whether they prefer training in the basic sciences or are preparing for a career in extension. Our education and research programs provide graduate and undergraduate students with unique hands-on learning opportunities using the latest technologies.

Essential Job Duties
The Department of Entomology and Plant Pathology in the NC State University College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (
CALS
) seeks to hire a Research Scholar to support a cooperative agreement (AP25PPQS&T00C172) focused on the development of advanced molecular diagnostics for plant quarantine pathogens. The position will contribute to the project titled
“Artificial intelligence, real-time
PCR
, and
CRISPR
methods for detecting Tomato yellow leaf curl virus exotic species in support of the
APHIS
PPQ
safeguarding mission.”
Under the general guidance of a Senior Scientist, the Research Scholar will lead laboratory-based research to develop, optimize, and validate AI-assisted real-time
PCR
and related molecular assays for the detection of exotic plant viruses, with an emphasis on Tomato yellow leaf curl virus. The incumbent will work independently to design experiments, analyze data, document results, and contribute to reports, publications, and technology transfer efforts in support of
APHIS
PPQ
diagnostic and safeguarding activities.
Development of Pathogen Detection Methods: Experimental Research, Data Analysis, and Reporting (80%)
Design, develop, optimize, and validate molecular diagnostic assays for quarantine plant pathogens, particularly plant viruses.
Perform
DNA
extraction and purification from plant materials, including seeds and leaf tissues.
Conduct in silico analyses of pathogen genomes, primers, and probes to support assay design and optimization.
Optimize real-time
PCR
reaction conditions and evaluate assay performance characteristics, including precision, accuracy, diagnostic sensitivity, and specificity.
Develop and validate synthetic reaction controls for molecular assays.
Prepare detailed work plans; execute experimental protocols; and generate validation, progress, and final project reports.
Maintain accurate experimental records in official laboratory notebooks and electronic data systems; analyze datasets and generate figures, summaries, and reports.
Operate, maintain, and troubleshoot specialized laboratory equipment, including QuantStudio™ 5 Real-Time
PCR
instruments,
PCR
thermocyclers, microplate readers, tissue grinders, and freeze-dryers.
Perform general laboratory duties, including preparation of culture media and buffers, inventory management, greenhouse plant maintenance, and routine lab maintenance.
Identify technical challenges, recommend improvements, and implement solutions to enhance assay quality and performance.
Contribute to scientific publications, technical reports, and draft manuscripts; co-present research findings with the Principal Investigator at meetings and conferences as appropriate.
Project Management, Quality Systems, and Training (20%)
Manage assigned project activities, including scheduling, task prioritization, and coordination with collaborators.
Track and monitor project-related budget expenditures.
Conduct all research in compliance with the
APHIS
PPQ
S&T
PPCDL
Quality Management System.
Develop and document work instructions and standard operating procedures for diagnostic platforms intended for use by field personnel.
Provide training and technical guidance to laboratory personnel; may oversee the day-to-day activities of a research assistant or biological laboratory technician.
You Belong Here! At NC State, our goal is for all employees to reach their fullest potential at work. As you consider this opportunity, we encourage you to review our
Employee Value Proposition
(
https://news.hr.ncsu.edu/you-belong-here/
) and learn more about what makes NC State the best place to learn and work for everyone.
Other Responsibilities
The incumbent will track reagents inventories and/or budget expenditures for the project; perform all activities with adherence to the Quality Management System (
QMS
) and approved Standard Operating Procedure (
SOP
) in the laboratory. Complete all required technical,
EEO
and work safety training.
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Ph.D in plant pathology, microbiology, molecular biology, biochemistry or related field (preferred), or MSc degree with more than 5 years’ experience supporting relevant research.
Other Required Qualifications
Documented experience with basic molecular biology methods (nucleic acid extraction,
PCR
and RT-
PCR
, automated gel electrophoresis and others), and fungi or bacterial culturing techniques.
Knowledge of detection & identification of microorganisms, and experience with sequencing and sequence analysis is preferred.
Ability to plan and follow planned activities independently,
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office
Good written and oral communication skills.
The candidate must be able to pass a background check.
The position is based at the
APHIS
PPQ
S&T Plant Pathogen Confirmatory Diagnostic Laboratory (
PPCDL
) in Laurel, MD. US citizenship or permanent residency is required.
Preferred Qualifications
Ph.D. degree with work experience of plant pathogen biology and/or detection, diagnostics of plant pathogens.
Knowledge of pathogen biology and/or detection & diagnostics of plant pathogens, mainly viruses, and experience with real-time RT-
PCR
, sequence analysis is preferred.
Required License(s) or Certification(s)
Valid NC driver’s license or ability to obtain one within 60 days of hire.
Valid NC Driver's License required
Yes
Commercial Driver's License required
No

Department

About the Department
The Department of Crop & Soil Sciences at North Carolina State University supports cutting-edge, multidisciplinary research programs that advance crop improvement, agricultural sustainability, and applied science across North Carolina and the United States. The department collaborates with university partners,
USDA
-
ARS
programs, and external stakeholders to translate scientific innovation into practical solutions that support breeders, growers, and agricultural industries across varied commodity systems.
Wolfpack Perks and Benefits

Essential Job Duties
The Research Scholar serves as a highly visible leader in the implementation of applied genomics and bioinformatics to support genomics-based selection decisions in breeding programs across the United States. This position plays a critical role in bridging research innovation with applied breeding outcomes by designing and deploying bioinformatics pipelines, coordinating multi-institutional breeding efforts, and enabling the integration of genomics into decision-making processes. The work directly impacts national breeding programs and contributes to improving crop performance, efficiency, and data-driven selection across multiple commodities.
Applied Genomics & Bioinformatics Leadership
Design, develop, and implement applied genomics strategies to support genomics-based selection decisions in public breeding programs.
Develop, integrate, and maintain bioinformatics pipelines and analytical tools for efficient and accurate genotyping across varied crop systems.
Perform large-scale genomic and phenotypic data analyses spanning multiple field programs and breeding populations.
Evaluate historical and current breeding program practices to establish standardized, scalable data management and analysis protocols.
Breeding Program Coordination & Collaboration
Coordinate applied genomics efforts across multiple university and
USDA
-
ARS
breeding programs, including cotton and peanut.
Collaborate with breeding teams to develop genomic resources and genotyping platforms aligned with program-specific breeding objectives.
Serve as a liaison among breeders, researchers, and partner institutions to ensure streamlined collaboration and consistent implementation of genomics tools.
Manage logistical procedures related to genomics implementation, data flow, and coordination across breeding programs.
Training, Communication & Stakeholder Engagement
Provide leadership, guidance, and training to breeders, research staff, and collaborators on applied genomics tools, data interpretation, and best practices.
Communicate genomics strategies, results, and impacts effectively to growers, breeders, stakeholders, and program leaders.
Support knowledge transfer and adoption of genomics-based breeding approaches through presentations, workshops, and collaborative discussions.
If you are a strategic scientific leader who thrives at the intersection of genomics, data science, and applied breeding and you are motivated by translating complex research into practical tools that strengthen national breeding programs we encourage you to apply and help drive the future of data-informed crop improvement.
Other Responsibilities
Qualifications
Minimum Education and Experience
Ph.D. genetics, bioinformatics, computational biology or relevant terminal degree), or relevant Master’s degree with at least 2 years of formal post-degree professional work experience, or in rare instances, an individual with a comparable professional experience but less than a master’s degree, plus 8 years of exceptional relevant professional experience, will be considered.
All degrees must be from appropriately accredited institutions.
Other Required Qualifications
Strong knowledge of informatics tools such as Linux, linkage mapping software, Python, R-statistical analysis software, and
SQL
or other databases.
Demonstrated leadership, mentorship, and interdisciplinary group collaboration skills with scientists from varied disciplines and backgrounds, including good interpersonal and clear English communication skills, both orally and in writing of reports and publications, effective work habits, and the ability to work as part of a team and in a self-directed manner.
Experience with the BreedBase database structure and experience with using it in an applied breeding program.
Preferred Qualifications
Strong knowledge of operations in breeding programs with at least 1 year post-PhD experience in an applied informatics program, proficiency in high-throughput bioinformatics analyses integrating phenotypic and genomic data, and demonstrated associated publications.
Experience in applied genomics in cotton and/or peanut.
Required License(s) or Certification(s)
n/a

No

No

is home to nearly 900 professionals who

Through this work, we help create spaces that empower students, faculty, and staff to reach their full potential.
